mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase corporate borrowing costs and discount future earnings, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate revenues and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances or unemployment spikes fail to predict earnings revisions within the horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized strength across consumer and housing demand channels, boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint signal shows no incremental predictive power over individual series or produces negative returns in >55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side resilience, which supports short-term equity momentum and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power during supply-constrained regimes or 5-day returns are negative in >60% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high consumer price index levels trigger expectations of prolonged monetary tightening, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: High inflation periods consistently yield positive equity returns or the threshold fails to separate return distributions.

mechanism_narrative: Strong non-farm payrolls growth combined with low unemployment confirms a tight labor market that sustains wage growth and consumer demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to predict earnings growth or yields negative returns in >50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects underlying economic expansion that supports corporate earnings trajectories, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth series shows zero correlation with 60-day forward returns or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production signals immediate supply-side weakness and inventory drawdowns, which reduces near-term revenue expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines are followed by positive equity returns in >65% of cases or the signal is subsumed by monetary policy moves.
